help.c(3) Library Functions Manual help.c(3)

NAME

help.c - Help text generation and formatting for options.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int options_config_calculate_max_col_width (const options_config_t *config)
Calculate global max column width across all help sections. void options_config_print_usage (const options_config_t *config, FILE *stream)
Print usage/help text. void options_config_print_usage_section (const options_config_t *config, FILE *stream)
Print only the USAGE section. void options_config_print_options_sections_with_width (const options_config_t *config, FILE *stream, int max_col_width, asciichat_mode_t mode)
Print everything except the USAGE section. void options_config_print_options_sections (const options_config_t *config, FILE *stream, asciichat_mode_t mode)
Print everything except the USAGE section (backward compatibility wrapper). void options_print_help_for_mode (const options_config_t *config, asciichat_mode_t mode, const char *program_name, const char *description, FILE *desc)
Print help for a specific mode or binary level. void options_struct_destroy (const options_config_t *config, void *options_struct)
Clean up memory owned by options struct.

Detailed Description

Help text generation and formatting for options.

Implements all help/usage text generation functions including programmatic section printers and unified help output.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Definition in file help.c.

Function Documentation

int options_config_calculate_max_col_width (const options_config_t * config)

Calculate global max column width across all help sections. Calculate global max column width for help output alignment.

Calculates the maximum width needed for proper alignment across USAGE, EXAMPLES, OPTIONS, and MODES sections.

Definition at line 75 of file help.c.

75                                                                            {
76   if (!config)
77     return 0;
78 
79   const char *binary_name = PLATFORM_BINARY_NAME;
80 
81   int max_col_width = 0;
82   char temp_buf[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM];
83 
84   // Check USAGE entries (capped at 45 chars for max first column)
85   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_usage_lines; i++) {
86     const usage_descriptor_t *usage = &config->usage_lines[i];
87     int len = 0;
88 
89     len += safe_snprintf(temp_buf + len, sizeof(temp_buf) - len, "%s", binary_name);
90 
91     if (usage->mode) {
92       const char *colored_mode = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, usage->mode);
93       len += safe_snprintf(temp_buf + len, sizeof(temp_buf) - len, " %s", colored_mode);
94     }
95 
96     if (usage->positional) {
97       const char *colored_pos = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, usage->positional);
98       len += safe_snprintf(temp_buf + len, sizeof(temp_buf) - len, " %s", colored_pos);
99     }
100 
101     if (usage->show_options) {
102       const char *options_text =
103           (usage->mode && strcmp(usage->mode, "<mode>") == 0) ? "[mode-options...]" : "[options...]";
104       const char *colored_opts = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, options_text);
105       len += safe_snprintf(temp_buf + len, sizeof(temp_buf) - len, " %s", colored_opts);
106     }
107 
108     int w = utf8_display_width(temp_buf);
109     if (w > LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH) {
110       w = LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH;
111     }
112     if (w > max_col_width)
113       max_col_width = w;
114   }
115 
116   // Check EXAMPLES entries
117   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_examples; i++) {
118     const example_descriptor_t *example = &config->examples[i];
119     int len = 0;
120 
121     // Only prepend program name if this is not a utility command
122     if (!example->is_utility_command) {
123       len += safe_snprintf(temp_buf + len, sizeof(temp_buf) - len, "%s", binary_name);
124 
125       // Programmatically add mode name based on mode_bitmask
126       const char *mode_name = get_mode_name_from_bitmask(example->mode_bitmask);
127       if (mode_name) {
128         len += safe_snprintf(temp_buf + len, sizeof(temp_buf) - len, " %s", mode_name);
129       }
130     }
131 
132     if (example->args) {
133       const char *colored_args = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, example->args);
134       len += safe_snprintf(temp_buf + len, sizeof(temp_buf) - len, " %s", colored_args);
135     }
136 
137     int w = utf8_display_width(temp_buf);
138     if (w > LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH) {
139       w = LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH;
140     }
141     if (w > max_col_width)
142       max_col_width = w;
143   }
144 
145   // Check MODES entries (capped at 45 chars)
146   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_modes; i++) {
147     const char *colored_name = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, config->modes[i].name);
148     int w = utf8_display_width(colored_name);
149     if (w > LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH) {
150       w = LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH;
151     }
152     if (w > max_col_width)
153       max_col_width = w;
154   }
155 
156   // Check OPTIONS entries (from descriptors)
157   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
158     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
159     if (desc->hide_from_mode_help || desc->hide_from_binary_help || !desc->group)
160       continue;
161 
162     // Build option display string with separate coloring for short and long flags
163     char opts_buf[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
164     if (desc->short_name && desc->short_name != '\0') {
165       char short_flag[16];
166       safe_snprintf(short_flag, sizeof(short_flag), "-%c", desc->short_name);
167       char long_flag[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
168       safe_snprintf(long_flag, sizeof(long_flag), "--%s", desc->long_name);
169       // Color short flag, add comma, color long flag
170       safe_snprintf(opts_buf, sizeof(opts_buf), "%s, %s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, short_flag),
171                     colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, long_flag));
172     } else {
173       char long_flag[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
174       safe_snprintf(long_flag, sizeof(long_flag), "--%s", desc->long_name);
175       safe_snprintf(opts_buf, sizeof(opts_buf), "%s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, long_flag));
176     }
177     const char *colored_opts = opts_buf;
178 
179     int w = utf8_display_width(colored_opts);
180     if (w > LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH) {
181       w = LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH;
182     }
183     if (w > max_col_width)
184       max_col_width = w;
185   }
186 
187   // Enforce maximum column width of 45 characters
188   if (max_col_width > 45) {
189     max_col_width = 45;
190   }
191 
192   return max_col_width;
193 }




References example_descriptor_t::args,
    BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM, BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL,
    colored_string(), options_config_t::descriptors,
    options_config_t::examples, option_descriptor_t::group,
    option_descriptor_t::hide_from_binary_help,
    option_descriptor_t::hide_from_mode_help,
    example_descriptor_t::is_utility_command, LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH,
    LOG_COLOR_FATAL, LOG_COLOR_INFO, LOG_COLOR_WARN,
    option_descriptor_t::long_name,
    example_descriptor_t::mode_bitmask, options_config_t::modes,
    help_mode_descriptor_t::name,
    options_config_t::num_descriptors,
    options_config_t::num_examples, options_config_t::num_modes,
    options_config_t::num_usage_lines, PLATFORM_BINARY_NAME,
    safe_snprintf(), option_descriptor_t::short_name,
    usage(), options_config_t::usage_lines, and
    utf8_display_width().


Referenced by
    options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(), and
    options_config_print_usage_section().






void
  options_config_print_options_sections (const options_config_t * config,
  FILE * stream, asciichat_mode_t mode)


Print everything except the USAGE section (backward compatibility
    wrapper). Print everything except the USAGE section (backward
    compatibility).




Calls options_config_print_options_sections_with_width with
    auto-calculation.


Definition at line 1071 of file help.c.


1071                                                                                                                 {
1072   options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(config, stream, 0, mode);
1073 }






References
    options_config_print_options_sections_with_width().






void
  options_config_print_options_sections_with_width (const
  options_config_t * config, FILE * stream, int max_col_width,
  asciichat_mode_t mode)


Print everything except the USAGE section. Prints MODES,
    MODE-OPTIONS, EXAMPLES, and OPTIONS sections. Used with
    options_config_print_usage_section to allow custom content in between.


Definition at line 880 of file help.c.


881                                                                              {
882   if (!config || !stream) {
883     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Config or stream is NULL");
884     return;
885   }
886 
887   // Detect terminal width - try actual terminal size first, fallback to COLUMNS env var
888   int term_width = 80;
889   terminal_size_t term_size;
890   if (terminal_get_size(&term_size) == ASCIICHAT_OK && term_size.cols > 40) {
891     term_width = term_size.cols;
892   } else {
893     const char *cols_env = SAFE_GETENV("COLUMNS");
894     if (cols_env) {
895       int cols = atoi(cols_env);
896       if (cols > 40)
897         term_width = cols;
898     }
899   }
900 
901   // Calculate column width if not provided
902   if (max_col_width <= 0) {
903     max_col_width = options_config_calculate_max_col_width(config);
904   }
905 
906   // CAP max_col_width: 86 if terminal wide, otherwise 45 for narrow first column
907   int max_col_cap = (term_width > 170) ? 86 : 45;
908   if (max_col_width > max_col_cap) {
909     max_col_width = max_col_cap;
910   }
911 
912   // Determine if this is binary-level help
913   // Discovery mode IS the binary-level help (shows binary options + discovery options)
914   // Other modes show only mode-specific options
915   bool for_binary_help = (mode == MODE_DISCOVERY);
916   // Build list of unique groups in order of first appearance
917   const char **unique_groups = SAFE_MALLOC(config->num_descriptors * sizeof(const char *), const char **);
918   size_t num_unique_groups = 0;
919 
920   // For binary-level help, we now use a two-pass system to order groups,
921   // ensuring binary-level option groups appear before discovery-mode groups.
922   if (for_binary_help) {
923     // Pass 1: Add GENERAL first, then LOGGING if present
924     bool general_added = false;
925     bool logging_added = false;
926     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
927       const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
928       if (desc->group) {
929         if (!general_added && strcmp(desc->group, "GENERAL") == 0) {
930           unique_groups[num_unique_groups++] = "GENERAL";
931           general_added = true;
932         }
933         if (!logging_added && strcmp(desc->group, "LOGGING") == 0) {
934           unique_groups[num_unique_groups++] = "LOGGING";
935           logging_added = true;
936         }
937         if (general_added && logging_added) {
938           break;
939         }
940       }
941     }
942 
943     // Pass 2: Collect all other groups that apply for binary help (all modes), if not already added.
944     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
945       const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
946       // An option applies if option_applies_to_mode says so for binary help (which checks OPTION_MODE_ALL)
947       if (option_applies_to_mode(desc, mode, for_binary_help) && desc->group) {
948         // Skip GENERAL and LOGGING groups if we already added them (for binary help)
949         if (strcmp(desc->group, "GENERAL") == 0 || strcmp(desc->group, "LOGGING") == 0) {
950           continue;
951         }
952 
953         bool group_exists = false;
954         for (size_t j = 0; j < num_unique_groups; j++) {
955           if (strcmp(unique_groups[j], desc->group) == 0) {
956             group_exists = true;
957             break;
958           }
959         }
960         if (!group_exists) {
961           unique_groups[num_unique_groups++] = desc->group;
962         }
963       } else if (desc->group) {
964       }
965     }
966   } else {
967     // Original logic for other modes
968     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
969       const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
970       if (option_applies_to_mode(desc, mode, for_binary_help) && desc->group) {
971         bool group_exists = false;
972         for (size_t j = 0; j < num_unique_groups; j++) {
973           if (strcmp(unique_groups[j], desc->group) == 0) {
974             group_exists = true;
975             break;
976           }
977         }
978         if (!group_exists) {
979           unique_groups[num_unique_groups++] = desc->group;
980         }
981       }
982     }
983   }
984 
985   // Print options grouped by category
986   for (size_t gi = 0; gi < num_unique_groups; gi++) {
987     const char *current_group = unique_groups[gi];
988     // Add newline before each group except the first
989     if (gi > 0) {
990       fprintf(stream, "\n");
991     }
992     fprintf(stream, "%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, current_group));
993 
994     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
995       const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
996       if (!option_applies_to_mode(desc, mode, for_binary_help) || !desc->group ||
997           strcmp(desc->group, current_group) != 0) {
998         continue;
999       }
1000 
1001       // Build option string (flag part) with separate coloring for short and long flags
1002       char colored_option_str[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM] = "";
1003       int colored_len = 0;
1004 
1005       // Short name and long name with separate coloring
1006       if (desc->short_name) {
1007         char short_flag[16];
1008         safe_snprintf(short_flag, sizeof(short_flag), "-%c", desc->short_name);
1009         char long_flag[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
1010         safe_snprintf(long_flag, sizeof(long_flag), "--%s", desc->long_name);
1011         // Color short flag, plain comma-space, color long flag
1012         colored_len +=
1013             safe_snprintf(colored_option_str + colored_len, sizeof(colored_option_str) - colored_len, "%s, %s",
1014                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, short_flag), colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, long_flag));
1015       } else {
1016         char long_flag[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
1017         safe_snprintf(long_flag, sizeof(long_flag), "--%s", desc->long_name);
1018         colored_len += safe_snprintf(colored_option_str + colored_len, sizeof(colored_option_str) - colored_len, "%s",
1019                                      colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, long_flag));
1020       }
1021 
1022       // Value placeholder (colored green)
1023       if (desc->type != OPTION_TYPE_BOOL && desc->type != OPTION_TYPE_ACTION) {
1024         colored_len += safe_snprintf(colored_option_str + colored_len, sizeof(colored_option_str) - colored_len, " ");
1025         const char *placeholder = get_option_help_placeholder_str(desc);
1026         if (placeholder[0] != '\0') {
1027           colored_len += safe_snprintf(colored_option_str + colored_len, sizeof(colored_option_str) - colored_len, "%s",
1028                                        colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, placeholder));
1029         }
1030       }
1031 
1032       // Build description with defaults and env vars
1033       char desc_str[1024] = "";
1034       int desc_len = 0;
1035 
1036       if (desc->help_text) {
1037         desc_len += safe_snprintf(desc_str + desc_len, sizeof(desc_str) - desc_len, "%s", desc->help_text);
1038       }
1039 
1040       // Skip adding default if the description already mentions it
1041       bool description_has_default =
1042           desc->help_text && (strstr(desc->help_text, "(default:") || strstr(desc->help_text, "=default)"));
1043 
1044       if (desc->default_value && !description_has_default) {
1045         char default_buf[256];
1046         int default_len = format_option_default_value_str(desc, default_buf, sizeof(default_buf));
1047         if (default_len > 0) {
1048           desc_len +=
1049               safe_snprintf(desc_str + desc_len, sizeof(desc_str) - desc_len, " (%s %s)",
1050                             colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "default:"), colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, default_buf));
1051         }
1052       }
1053 
1054       if (desc->required) {
1055         desc_len += safe_snprintf(desc_str + desc_len, sizeof(desc_str) - desc_len, " [REQUIRED]");
1056       }
1057 
1058       layout_print_two_column_row(stream, colored_option_str, desc_str, max_col_width, term_width, 2);
1059     }
1060   }
1061 
1062   // Cleanup
1063   SAFE_FREE(unique_groups);
1064 }

    BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL, colored_string(),
    terminal_size_t::cols, option_descriptor_t::default_value,
    options_config_t::descriptors, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    format_option_default_value_str(),
    get_option_help_placeholder_str(), option_descriptor_t::group,
    option_descriptor_t::help_text, layout_print_two_column_row(),
    LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, LOG_COLOR_FATAL, LOG_COLOR_INFO,
    LOG_COLOR_WARN, option_descriptor_t::long_name,
    MODE_DISCOVERY, options_config_t::num_descriptors,
    option_applies_to_mode(), OPTION_TYPE_ACTION,
    OPTION_TYPE_BOOL, options_config_calculate_max_col_width(),
    option_descriptor_t::required, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_GETENV,

    option_descriptor_t::short_name, terminal_get_size(), and

Referenced by options_config_print_options_sections(), and
    options_print_help_for_mode().






void
  options_config_print_usage (const options_config_t * config, FILE *
  stream)


Print usage/help text. Generates formatted help with grouped
    options.




Parameters


Config Module Options
  configuration


stream Output stream (stdout or stderr)


Definition at line 704 of file help.c.


704                                                                               {
705   if (!config || !stream)
706     return;
707 
708   // Detect terminal width from COLUMNS env var or use default
709   int term_width = 80;
710   const char *cols_env = SAFE_GETENV("COLUMNS");
711   if (cols_env) {
712     int cols = atoi(cols_env);
713     if (cols > 40)
714       term_width = cols;
715   }
716 
717   // Binary-level help uses MODE_DISCOVERY internally
718   asciichat_mode_t mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
719   bool for_binary_help = true;
720 
721   // Calculate per-section column widths (each section is independent, capped at 75)
722   int usage_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "usage", mode, for_binary_help);
723   int modes_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "modes", mode, for_binary_help);
724   int examples_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "examples", mode, for_binary_help);
725   int options_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "options", mode, for_binary_help);
726 
727   // Print programmatically generated sections (USAGE, MODES, MODE-OPTIONS, EXAMPLES)
728   print_usage_section(config, stream, term_width, usage_max_col_width);
729   print_modes_section(config, stream, term_width, modes_max_col_width);
730   print_mode_options_section(stream, term_width, 40); // Keep reasonable width for mode-options
731   print_examples_section(config, stream, term_width, examples_max_col_width, MODE_SERVER, true);
732 
733   // Build list of unique groups in order of first appearance
734 
735   const char **unique_groups = SAFE_MALLOC(config->num_descriptors * sizeof(const char *), const char **);
736   size_t num_unique_groups = 0;
737 
738   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
739     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
740     // Filter by mode_bitmask - for binary help, show binary options
741     if (!option_applies_to_mode(desc, MODE_SERVER, for_binary_help) || !desc->group) {
742       continue;
743     }
744 
745     // Check if this group is already in the list
746     bool group_exists = false;
747     for (size_t j = 0; j < num_unique_groups; j++) {
748       if (unique_groups[j] && strcmp(unique_groups[j], desc->group) == 0) {
749         group_exists = true;
750         break;
751       }
752     }
753 
754     // Add new group to list
755     if (!group_exists && num_unique_groups < config->num_descriptors) {
756       unique_groups[num_unique_groups++] = desc->group;
757     }
758   }
759 
760   // Print options grouped by group name
761   for (size_t g = 0; g < num_unique_groups; g++) {
762     const char *current_group = unique_groups[g];
763     // Only add leading newline for groups after the first one
764     if (g > 0) {
765       fprintf(stream, "\n");
766     }
767     fprintf(stream, "%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, current_group));
768 
769     // Print all options in this group
770     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
771       const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
772 
773       // Skip if not in current group or if doesn't apply to mode
774       if (!option_applies_to_mode(desc, MODE_SERVER, for_binary_help) || !desc->group ||
775           strcmp(desc->group, current_group) != 0) {
776         continue;
777       }
778 
779       // Build option string with separate coloring for short and long flags
780       char option_str[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM] = "";
781       int option_len = 0;
782 
783       // Short name and long name with separate coloring
784       if (desc->short_name) {
785         char short_flag[16];
786         safe_snprintf(short_flag, sizeof(short_flag), "-%c", desc->short_name);
787         char long_flag[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
788         safe_snprintf(long_flag, sizeof(long_flag), "--%s", desc->long_name);
789         // Color short flag, plain comma-space, color long flag
790         option_len +=
791             safe_snprintf(option_str + option_len, sizeof(option_str) - option_len, "%s, %s",
792                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, short_flag), colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, long_flag));
793       } else {
794         char long_flag[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
795         safe_snprintf(long_flag, sizeof(long_flag), "--%s", desc->long_name);
796         option_len += safe_snprintf(option_str + option_len, sizeof(option_str) - option_len, "%s",
797                                     colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, long_flag));
798       }
799 
800       // Value placeholder (colored green)
801       if (desc->type != OPTION_TYPE_BOOL && desc->type != OPTION_TYPE_ACTION) {
802         option_len += safe_snprintf(option_str + option_len, sizeof(option_str) - option_len, " ");
803         const char *placeholder = get_option_help_placeholder_str(desc);
804         if (placeholder[0] != '\0') {
805           option_len += safe_snprintf(option_str + option_len, sizeof(option_str) - option_len, "%s",
806                                       colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, placeholder));
807         }
808       }
809 
810       // Build description string (plain text, colors applied when printing)
811       char desc_str[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM] = "";
812       int desc_len = 0;
813 
814       if (desc->help_text) {
815         desc_len += safe_snprintf(desc_str + desc_len, sizeof(desc_str) - desc_len, "%s", desc->help_text);
816       }
817 
818       // Skip adding default if the description already mentions it
819       bool description_has_default =
820           desc->help_text && (strstr(desc->help_text, "(default:") || strstr(desc->help_text, "=default)"));
821 
822       if (desc->default_value && !description_has_default) {
823         char default_buf[256];
824         int default_len = format_option_default_value_str(desc, default_buf, sizeof(default_buf));
825         if (default_len > 0) {
826           desc_len +=
827               safe_snprintf(desc_str + desc_len, sizeof(desc_str) - desc_len, " (%s %s)",
828                             colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "default:"), colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, default_buf));
829         }
830       }
831 
832       if (desc->required) {
833         desc_len += safe_snprintf(desc_str + desc_len, sizeof(desc_str) - desc_len, " [REQUIRED]");
834       }
835 
836       // Use layout function with section-specific column width for consistent alignment
837       // option_str already contains colored_string() results, so pass it directly
838       layout_print_two_column_row(stream, option_str, desc_str, options_max_col_width, term_width, 2);
839     }
840   }
841 
842   // Cleanup
843   SAFE_FREE(unique_groups);
844 
845   fprintf(stream, "\n");
846 }






References BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM, BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL,
    colored_string(), option_descriptor_t::default_value,
    options_config_t::descriptors,
    format_option_default_value_str(),
    get_option_help_placeholder_str(), option_descriptor_t::group,
    option_descriptor_t::help_text, layout_print_two_column_row(),
    LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, LOG_COLOR_FATAL, LOG_COLOR_INFO,
    LOG_COLOR_WARN, option_descriptor_t::long_name,
    MODE_DISCOVERY, MODE_SERVER,
    options_config_t::num_descriptors, option_applies_to_mode(),
    OPTION_TYPE_ACTION, OPTION_TYPE_BOOL,
    option_descriptor_t::required, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_GETENV,
    SAFE_MALLOC, safe_snprintf(),
    option_descriptor_t::short_name, and
    option_descriptor_t::type.






void
  options_config_print_usage_section (const options_config_t * config,
  FILE * stream)


Print only the USAGE section. Splits usage printing from other
    sections to allow custom content (like positional argument examples) to be
    inserted between USAGE and other sections.


Definition at line 854 of file help.c.


854                                                                                       {
855   if (!config || !stream)
856     return;
857 
858   // Detect terminal width from COLUMNS env var or use default
859   int term_width = 80;
860   const char *cols_env = SAFE_GETENV("COLUMNS");
861   if (cols_env) {
862     int cols = atoi(cols_env);
863     if (cols > 40)
864       term_width = cols;
865   }
866 
867   // Calculate global max column width across all sections for consistent alignment
868   int max_col_width = options_config_calculate_max_col_width(config);
869 
870   // Print only USAGE section
871   print_usage_section(config, stream, term_width, max_col_width);
872 }






References options_config_calculate_max_col_width(), and
    SAFE_GETENV.






void
  options_print_help_for_mode (const options_config_t * config,
  asciichat_mode_t mode, const char * program_name, const char *
  description, FILE * desc)


Print help for a specific mode or binary level. Print help for a
    specific mode or binary level (unified function).




This is the single unified function for all help output (binary
    level and all modes). It handles common layout logic, terminal detection,
    and section printing.




Parameters


Config Module Options config with
  all options (binary + all modes)


mode Mode to show help for (use -1 for binary-level help)


program_name Full program name with mode (e.g., "ascii-chat
  server")


description Brief description of the mode/binary


desc Output file stream (usually stdout)


Definition at line 1091 of file help.c.


1092                                                                       {
1093   if (!config || !desc) {
1094     if (desc) {
1095       fprintf(desc, "Error: Failed to create options config\n");
1096     } else {
1097       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Config or desc is NULL");
1098     }
1099     return;
1100   }
1101 
1102   // Detect terminal width early so we can decide whether to show ASCII art
1103   int term_width = 80;
1104   terminal_size_t term_size;
1105   if (terminal_get_size(&term_size) == ASCIICHAT_OK && term_size.cols > 40) {
1106     term_width = term_size.cols;
1107   } else {
1108     const char *cols_env = SAFE_GETENV("COLUMNS");
1109     if (cols_env) {
1110       int cols = atoi(cols_env);
1111       if (cols > 40)
1112         term_width = cols;
1113     }
1114   }
1115 
1116   // Print ASCII art logo only if terminal is wide enough (ASCII art is ~52 chars wide)
1117   if (term_width >= 60) {
1118     (void)fprintf(desc, "  __ _ ___  ___(_|_)       ___| |__   __ _| |_ \n");
1119     (void)fprintf(desc, " / _` / __|/ __| | |_____ / __| '_ \\ / _` | __|\n");
1120     (void)fprintf(desc, "| (_| \\__ \\ (__| | |_____| (__| | | | (_| | |_ \n");
1121     (void)fprintf(desc, " \\__,_|___/\\___|_|_|      \\___|_| |_|\\__,_|\\__|\n");
1122     (void)fprintf(desc, "\n");
1123   }
1124 
1125   // Print program name and description (color mode name magenta if it's a mode-specific help)
1126   if (program_name) {
1127     const char *space = strchr(program_name, ' ');
1128     if (space && mode >= 0) {
1129       // Mode-specific help: color the mode name
1130       int binary_len = space - program_name;
1131       (void)fprintf(desc, "%.*s %s - %s\n\n", binary_len, program_name, colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, space + 1),
1132                     description);
1133     } else {
1134       // Binary-level help: use colored_string for the program name too
1135       (void)fprintf(desc, "%s - %s\n\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, program_name), description);
1136     }
1137   }
1138 
1139   // Print project links
1140   print_project_links(desc);
1141   (void)fprintf(desc, "\n");
1142 
1143   // Determine if this is binary-level help (called for 'ascii-chat --help')
1144   // Binary help uses MODE_DISCOVERY as the mode value
1145   bool for_binary_help = (mode == MODE_DISCOVERY);
1146 
1147   // Calculate column widths for USAGE upfront (before printing) for alignment
1148   int usage_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "usage", mode, for_binary_help);
1149   // Use USAGE width for both USAGE and EXAMPLES sections for consistent alignment
1150   // (don't let long examples push column too far right)
1151   int usage_examples_col_width = usage_max_col_width;
1152 
1153   // Print USAGE section (with section-specific column width and mode filtering)
1154   fprintf(desc, "%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, "USAGE"));
1155   if (config->num_usage_lines > 0) {
1156     // Get mode name for filtering usage lines
1157     const char *mode_name = NULL;
1158     switch (mode) {
1159     case MODE_SERVER:
1160       mode_name = "server";
1161       break;
1162     case MODE_CLIENT:
1163       mode_name = "client";
1164       break;
1165     case MODE_MIRROR:
1166       mode_name = "mirror";
1167       break;
1168     case MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE:
1169       mode_name = "discovery-service";
1170       break;
1171     case MODE_DISCOVERY:
1172     default:
1173       mode_name = NULL; // Binary help shows all usage lines
1174       break;
1175     }
1176 
1177     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_usage_lines; i++) {
1178       const usage_descriptor_t *usage = &config->usage_lines[i];
1179 
1180       // Filter usage lines by mode
1181       if (!for_binary_help) {
1182         // For mode-specific help, show the current mode's usage line and the generic [mode] --help line
1183         bool is_current_mode = (usage->mode && mode_name && strcmp(usage->mode, mode_name) == 0);
1184         bool is_generic_help_line =
1185             (!usage->mode && usage->positional && strcmp(usage->positional, "[mode] --help") == 0);
1186 
1187         if (!is_current_mode && !is_generic_help_line) {
1188           continue;
1189         }
1190       }
1191 
1192       char usage_buf[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM];
1193       int len = 0;
1194 
1195       len += safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, "ascii-chat");
1196 
1197       // For mode-specific help, use [mode] placeholder when showing --help usage
1198       if (usage->mode) {
1199         if (!for_binary_help && usage->positional && strcmp(usage->positional, "--help") == 0) {
1200           // Mode-specific help: show [mode] --help as generic placeholder
1201           len +=
1202               safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, " %s", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "[mode]"));
1203         } else {
1204           // Binary help or non-help usage: show actual mode name
1205           len += safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, " %s",
1206                                colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, usage->mode));
1207         }
1208       }
1209 
1210       if (usage->positional) {
1211         len += safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, " %s",
1212                              colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, usage->positional));
1213       }
1214 
1215       if (usage->show_options) {
1216         const char *options_text =
1217             (usage->mode && strcmp(usage->mode, "<mode>") == 0) ? "[mode-options...]" : "[options...]";
1218         len += safe_snprintf(usage_buf + len, sizeof(usage_buf) - len, " %s",
1219                              colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, options_text));
1220       }
1221 
1222       layout_print_two_column_row(desc, usage_buf, usage->description, usage_examples_col_width, term_width, 0);
1223     }
1224   }
1225   fprintf(desc, "\n");
1226 
1227   // Print MODES section (only for binary-level help)
1228   if (for_binary_help && config->num_modes > 0) {
1229     int modes_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "modes", mode, for_binary_help);
1230     print_modes_section(config, desc, term_width, modes_max_col_width);
1231   }
1232 
1233   // Print positional argument examples (with mode filtering and section-specific column width)
1234   if (config->num_positional_args > 0) {
1235     // First, check if any positional args apply to this mode
1236     option_mode_bitmask_t current_mode_bitmask = 1U << mode;
1237     bool has_applicable_positional_args = false;
1238 
1239     for (size_t pa_idx = 0; pa_idx < config->num_positional_args; pa_idx++) {
1240       const positional_arg_descriptor_t *pos_arg = &config->positional_args[pa_idx];
1241 
1242       // Filter by mode_bitmask (matching the parsing code logic)
1243       if (pos_arg->mode_bitmask != 0 && !(pos_arg->mode_bitmask & current_mode_bitmask)) {
1244         continue;
1245       }
1246 
1247       if (pos_arg->section_heading && pos_arg->examples && pos_arg->num_examples > 0) {
1248         has_applicable_positional_args = true;
1249         break;
1250       }
1251     }
1252 
1253     // Only print the section if there are applicable positional args
1254     if (has_applicable_positional_args) {
1255       int positional_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "positional", mode, false);
1256 
1257       for (size_t pa_idx = 0; pa_idx < config->num_positional_args; pa_idx++) {
1258         const positional_arg_descriptor_t *pos_arg = &config->positional_args[pa_idx];
1259 
1260         // Filter by mode_bitmask (matching the parsing code logic)
1261         if (pos_arg->mode_bitmask != 0 && !(pos_arg->mode_bitmask & current_mode_bitmask)) {
1262           continue;
1263         }
1264 
1265         if (pos_arg->section_heading && pos_arg->examples && pos_arg->num_examples > 0) {
1266           (void)fprintf(desc, "%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, pos_arg->section_heading));
1267 
1268           for (size_t i = 0; i < pos_arg->num_examples; i++) {
1269             const char *example = pos_arg->examples[i];
1270             const char *p = example;
1271             const char *desc_start = NULL;
1272 
1273             while (*p == ' ')
1274               p++;
1275             const char *first_part = p;
1276 
1277             while (*p && !(*p == ' ' && *(p + 1) == ' '))
1278               p++;
1279             int first_len_bytes = (int)(p - first_part);
1280 
1281             while (*p == ' ')
1282               p++;
1283             if (*p) {
1284               desc_start = p;
1285             }
1286 
1287             char colored_first_part[256];
1288             safe_snprintf(colored_first_part, sizeof(colored_first_part), "%.*s", first_len_bytes, first_part);
1289             char colored_result[512];
1290             safe_snprintf(colored_result, sizeof(colored_result), "%s",
1291                           colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, colored_first_part));
1292 
1293             layout_print_two_column_row(desc, colored_result, desc_start ? desc_start : "", positional_max_col_width,
1294                                         term_width, 0);
1295           }
1296           (void)fprintf(desc, "\n");
1297         }
1298       }
1299     }
1300   }
1301 
1302   // Print EXAMPLES section (using USAGE column width for alignment)
1303   print_examples_section(config, desc, term_width, usage_examples_col_width, mode, for_binary_help);
1304 
1305   // Print custom sections (after EXAMPLES, before OPTIONS)
1306   if (config->num_custom_sections > 0) {
1307     option_mode_bitmask_t current_mode_bitmask = 1U << mode;
1308     for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_custom_sections; i++) {
1309       const custom_section_descriptor_t *section = &config->custom_sections[i];
1310 
1311       // Filter by mode_bitmask
1312       if (section->mode_bitmask != 0 && !(section->mode_bitmask & current_mode_bitmask)) {
1313         continue;
1314       }
1315 
1316       if (section->heading) {
1317         fprintf(desc, "%s\n", colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, section->heading));
1318       }
1319 
1320       if (section->content) {
1321         // Special handling for KEYBINDINGS section: colorize keybindings and wrap to 70 chars
1322         if (section->heading && strcmp(section->heading, "KEYBINDINGS") == 0) {
1323           // Build colored version with proper keybinding colorization
1324           char colored_output[2048] = "";
1325           const char *src = section->content;
1326           char *dst = colored_output;
1327           size_t remaining = sizeof(colored_output) - 1;
1328 
1329           while (*src && remaining > 0) {
1330             // Colorize ? that don't end sentences
1331             if (*src == '?' && *(src + 1) != '\n' && *(src + 1) != '\0') {
1332               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "?");
1333               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1334               if (len <= remaining) {
1335                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1336                 dst += len;
1337                 remaining -= len;
1338                 src += 1;
1339               } else {
1340                 break;
1341               }
1342             } else if (strncmp(src, "Space", 5) == 0 &&
1343                        (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' '))) {
1344               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "Space");
1345               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1346               if (len <= remaining) {
1347                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1348                 dst += len;
1349                 remaining -= len;
1350                 src += 5;
1351               } else {
1352                 break;
1353               }
1354             } else if (strncmp(src, "arrows", 6) == 0 &&
1355                        (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' '))) {
1356               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "arrows");
1357               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1358               if (len <= remaining) {
1359                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1360                 dst += len;
1361                 remaining -= len;
1362                 src += 6;
1363               } else {
1364                 break;
1365               }
1366             } else if (*src == 'm' && (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' ')) &&
1367                        (*(src + 1) == ',' || *(src + 1) == ')')) {
1368               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "m");
1369               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1370               if (len <= remaining) {
1371                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1372                 dst += len;
1373                 remaining -= len;
1374                 src += 1;
1375               } else {
1376                 break;
1377               }
1378             } else if (*src == 'c' && (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' ')) &&
1379                        (*(src + 1) == ',' || *(src + 1) == ')')) {
1380               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "c");
1381               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1382               if (len <= remaining) {
1383                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1384                 dst += len;
1385                 remaining -= len;
1386                 src += 1;
1387               } else {
1388                 break;
1389               }
1390             } else if (*src == 'f' && (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' ')) &&
1391                        (*(src + 1) == ',' || *(src + 1) == ')')) {
1392               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "f");
1393               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1394               if (len <= remaining) {
1395                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1396                 dst += len;
1397                 remaining -= len;
1398                 src += 1;
1399               } else {
1400                 break;
1401               }
1402             } else if (*src == 'r' && (src > section->content && (*(src - 1) == ',' || *(src - 1) == ' ')) &&
1403                        (*(src + 1) == ')')) {
1404               const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, "r");
1405               size_t len = strlen(colored);
1406               if (len <= remaining) {
1407                 memcpy(dst, colored, len);
1408                 dst += len;
1409                 remaining -= len;
1410                 src += 1;
1411               } else {
1412                 break;
1413               }
1414             } else {
1415               *dst++ = *src++;
1416               remaining--;
1417             }
1418           }
1419           *dst = '\0';
1420           // Print with 2-space indent, wrapping at min(terminal width, 90)
1421           fprintf(desc, "  ");
1422           int keybindings_wrap_width = term_width < 90 ? term_width : 90;
1423           layout_print_wrapped_description(desc, colored_output, 2, keybindings_wrap_width, 0);
1424           fprintf(desc, "\n");
1425         } else {
1426           fprintf(desc, "%s\n", section->content);
1427         }
1428       }
1429 
1430       fprintf(desc, "\n");
1431     }
1432   }
1433 
1434   // Print options sections (with section-specific column width for options)
1435   int options_max_col_width = calculate_section_max_col_width(config, "options", mode, for_binary_help);
1436   options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(config, desc, options_max_col_width, mode);
1437 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM,
    colored_string(), terminal_size_t::cols,
    custom_section_descriptor_t::content,
    options_config_t::custom_sections, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    positional_arg_descriptor_t::examples,
    custom_section_descriptor_t::heading,
    layout_print_two_column_row(),
    layout_print_wrapped_description(), LOG_COLOR_DEBUG,
    LOG_COLOR_FATAL, LOG_COLOR_INFO, LOG_COLOR_WARN,
    custom_section_descriptor_t::mode_bitmask,
    positional_arg_descriptor_t::mode_bitmask, MODE_CLIENT,
    MODE_DISCOVERY, MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, MODE_MIRROR,
    MODE_SERVER, options_config_t::num_custom_sections,
    positional_arg_descriptor_t::num_examples,
    options_config_t::num_modes,
    options_config_t::num_positional_args,
    options_config_t::num_usage_lines,
    options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(),
    options_config_t::positional_args, print_project_links(),
    SAFE_GETENV, safe_snprintf(),
    positional_arg_descriptor_t::section_heading, SET_ERRNO,
    terminal_get_size(), usage(), and
    options_config_t::usage_lines.


Referenced by usage().






void
  options_struct_destroy (const options_config_t * config, void *
  options_struct)


Clean up memory owned by options struct. Frees all auto-duplicated
    strings and sets pointers to NULL. Call this before freeing the options
    struct.




Parameters


Config Module Options
  configuration


options_struct Options struct to clean up


Definition at line 1439 of file help.c.


1439                                                                                   {
1440   if (!config || !options_struct)
1441     return;
1442 
1443   // Free all owned strings
1444   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_owned_strings; i++) {
1445     free(config->owned_strings[i]);
1446   }
1447 
1448   // Reset owned strings tracking
1449   ((options_config_t *)config)->num_owned_strings = 0;
1450 
1451   // NULL out string fields
1452   char *base = (char *)options_struct;
1453   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
1454     const option_descriptor_t *desc = &config->descriptors[i];
1455     if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_STRING && desc->owns_memory) {
1456       char **field = (char **)(base + desc->offset);
1457       *field = NULL;
1458     }
1459   }
1460 }






References options_config_t::descriptors,
    options_config_t::num_descriptors,
    options_config_t::num_owned_strings,
    option_descriptor_t::offset, OPTION_TYPE_STRING,
    options_config_t::owned_strings,
    option_descriptor_t::owns_memory, and
    option_descriptor_t::type.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.12
    ascii-chat
